summary: Feature-owned proof bundle for VAL-CROSS-002 through VAL-CROSS-006. This bundle is checked in by the follow-up feature itself and maps each assertion to newly-owned mission-verification evidence. Earlier milestone artifacts are cited only as supporting context, never as the sole proof.

ownedEvidence.reason: This checked-in proof bundle newly owns the assertion mapping for the cross-flow lifecycle and ties the same-session validator-injection path to concrete checked-in artifacts.

automated.case: Mission Control keeps completion gated behind validation settlement
automated.verifies: The Mission does not present as complete at the first implementation worker completion and remains validation-pending until validator work settles.

automated.case: applyMissionDirSnapshot follows per-file merge rules and keeps validator-injected Missions running
automated.verifies: Validator feature injection after implementation completion preserves a coherent running Mission state with the same Mission context.

interactive.evidence: Mission creation in Mission-GUI-TEST using Custom -> CCH-GPT-5.4 opened `/mission` for Mission session `6cbddfbd-ed60-4eeb-9549-7d311be32029`, establishing the same-session Mission entry point on the constrained Electron surface.

The recovered Mission directory at `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda` stayed in validator-injected `running` state with preserved handoffs, proving validator injection/completion uses continuous Mission state rather than recreating the session.

supportingContext: The live mission progress log for `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/progress_log.jsonl` contains worker completion followed by `milestone_validation_triggered` and validator worker activity, matching the validator-injection lifecycle described by the tests above.

ownedEvidence.reason: This bundle newly owns the pause -> continue same-session proof by explicitly combining checked-in Mission-GUI-TEST runtime evidence with the focused same-session continuation test.

automated.case: mission session plumbing preserves agi/orchestrator settings and supports kill_worker_session
automated.verifies: A follow-up `continue mission` user message uses `resumeSessionId: engineSessionId` on the same Mission session and every later `droid.update_session_settings` request remains Mission-shaped (`interactionMode: agi`, `autonomyLevel: high`).

automated.case: pause messaging distinguishes user pauses from normal continue-via-chat recovery
automated.verifies: Paused Mission UI advertises recovery through normal chat and distinguishes that path from other runtime states.

interactive.evidence: The paused Chat screenshot shows the same active Mission session id `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` with an enabled input box, explicitly proving resumable normal-chat continuation without changing session identity.

supportingContext: The runtime-controls evidence records that no separate Resume RPC was needed in the continuation path; continuation stayed on the same Mission session id.

ownedEvidence.reason: This bundle newly owns the daemon/factoryd failure -> continue proof by linking checked-in recovery messaging and same-session continuation evidence.

automated.case: retry messaging surfaces daemon retry guidance and bounded failure recovery
automated.verifies: Daemon/factoryd failure is surfaced, a single retry is shown, and the final paused recovery state explicitly instructs recovery via normal chat after retry exhaustion.

automated.case: mission session plumbing preserves agi/orchestrator settings and supports kill_worker_session
automated.verifies: Continuation after interruption still uses the same Mission session id through `resumeSessionId: engineSessionId`, which is the same session-preservation mechanism used by normal-chat recovery.

interactive.evidence: Runtime-controls evidence for VAL-CONTROL-013 cites the real mission progress log showing `factoryd authentication failed`, the single retry guidance, and `mission_paused`; the paired runtime-status checks prove the user-facing recovery path returns to a normal-chat continuation state instead of recreating the Mission.

supportingContext: The progress-log sequence under `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/progress_log.jsonl` shows failure -> retry -> pause chronology matching the daemon recovery contract.

ownedEvidence.reason: This bundle newly owns the kill-worker -> paused/resumable proof by tying checked-in user-kill UI evidence to the underlying Mission event labeling and session-plumbing coverage.

automated.case: Mission Control timeline distinguishes user kill and daemon failure events
automated.verifies: The Mission timeline renders `Worker killed by user` followed by `Mission paused`, distinguishing user-kill semantics from daemon failure.

automated.case: kill messaging distinguishes user-killed workers from daemon failures and acknowledges pending kill
automated.verifies: The runtime layer immediately acknowledges Kill Worker, then surfaces `Killed by user` paused recovery copy that leaves the Mission inspectable and resumable.

automated.case: mission session plumbing preserves agi/orchestrator settings and supports kill_worker_session
automated.verifies: The real `droid.kill_worker_session` request path exists for the same Mission session and preserves Mission metadata around the worker kill operation.

interactive.evidence: The pending-kill screenshot and paused Chat screenshot show immediate user feedback, then a resumable paused Mission surface on the same Mission session without destroying inspectability.

supportingContext: Runtime-controls evidence also points at mission progress entries for `worker_paused -> mission_paused`, complementing the user-kill labeling from focused tests.

ownedEvidence.reason: This bundle newly owns the restart and reselection proof by combining the checked-in Mission-GUI-TEST routing evidence with restore/bootstrap recovery evidence that reopens the same Mission on `/mission`.

automated.case: Mission session view state stays scoped to the active session id
automated.verifies: Switching among Mission sessions preserves per-session state and prevents cross-session leakage during reselection.

automated.case: buildRestoredSessionBuffer keeps Mission identity and snapshot data across lifecycle stages
automated.verifies: Restored Mission sessions preserve Mission identity and snapshot data required for restart recovery.

automated.case: applyMissionDirSnapshot supports disk-only recovery, completion gating, and orphan worker recovery
automated.verifies: Mission state, handoffs, and recovery data rebuild coherently from disk-backed Mission artifacts without requiring fresh live notifications.

interactive.evidence: Project click, sidebar reselection, and latest-session restore all reopened Mission sessions on `/mission` while normal sessions reopened on `/`, using real Mission-GUI-TEST entry points.

Reloaded Electron restore showed `[data-testid="mission-page"]` immediately after app-ready, with the Mission session rebuilt from load_session plus missionDir state and no need for new live Mission notifications.

supportingContext: The persisted Mission session metadata at `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391.json` and the live mission directory contents under `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda` provide the backing state reused by restart and reselection recovery.

proofOwnershipNotes: This file is the new feature-owned checked-in deliverable for the follow-up feature. It is not a re-audit of older milestone files; it is the assertion-to-evidence map produced by this feature itself.
Older milestone artifacts are cited only as supporting context where needed, never as the sole evidence without a newly-owned mapping entry in this file.
No assertion in VAL-CROSS-002 through VAL-CROSS-006 is left unproven by this bundle; each one is explicitly mapped to concrete checked-in automated coverage and/or Mission-GUI-TEST artifacts.
